skill.name: gws-calendar-agenda
skill.description: Google Calendar: Show upcoming events across all calendars.
skill.summary: View upcoming Google Calendar events with flexible time range filtering.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation file that describes usage of the official Google Workspace CLI tool. All static analysis findings are false positives - the flagged patterns appear in markdown documentation (code blocks and relative links), not executable code. The skill invokes a pre-approved binary (gws) with read-only operations.

content.value_statement: Quickly see upcoming events across all your calendars without opening a browser. Filter by date range or specific calendar with simple command flags.
actual_capabilities: Display upcoming events from all Google Calendars
Filter events by time range (today, tomorrow, week, custom days)
Filter events by specific calendar name or ID
Override timezone with IANA timezone format
Output in multiple formats including table view
limitations: Requires the gws CLI binary to be installed and authenticated
Read-only access - cannot create, modify, or delete events
Requires Google account authentication via gws-shared setup
Depends on Google Calendar API availability

use_cases.title: Daily standup preparation
use_cases.description: Quickly view today's meetings before team standup to prepare for scheduled calls.

use_cases.title: Executive schedule review
use_cases.description: Review the week's agenda to prioritize meetings and identify scheduling conflicts.

use_cases.title: Travel planning
use_cases.description: Check upcoming events across multiple calendars before booking travel to avoid conflicts.

prompt_templates.title: Basic agenda view
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my upcoming calendar events for the next few days.

prompt_templates.title: Today's events only
prompt_templates.prompt: What events do I have scheduled for today?

prompt_templates.title: Week ahead review
prompt_templates.prompt: Display all calendar events for this week in a table format.

prompt_templates.title: Specific calendar filter with timezone
prompt_templates.prompt: Show the next 5 days of events from my Work calendar only, using Pacific timezone.
prompt_templates.scenario: Filtering for specific calendar while traveling

output: 09:00 AM - Team Standup (30 min) - Work Calendar
02:00 PM - Client Call (1 hr) - Work Calendar
06:00 PM - Dinner with Friends (2 hr) - Personal Calendar

output_examples.input: Show this week's events in table format
output: | Mon Apr 5  | 9:00 AM   | Team Standup         | Work           |
| Mon Apr 5  | 2:00 PM   | Client Call          | Work           |
| Wed Apr 7  | 10:00 AM  | Sprint Planning      | Work           |
| Fri Apr 9  | 4:00 PM   | All Hands            | Company        |

best_practices: Use --today or --tomorrow for quick daily planning instead of specifying --days 1
Filter by --calendar when you have multiple calendars to reduce noise
Specify --timezone when traveling to see events in local time
anti_patterns: Do not use --days with very large values (e.g., 365) as output will be overwhelming
Avoid querying without filters if you have many calendars with frequent events
Do not rely on default timezone when traveling across time zones

faq.question: Do I need to install the gws CLI separately?
faq.answer: Yes, the gws CLI must be installed and authenticated. Follow the gws-shared skill setup instructions for initial configuration.

faq.question: Can this skill create or modify calendar events?
faq.answer: No, this skill is read-only. It only displays existing events. Use other gws-calendar commands for creating or modifying events.

faq.question: How do I see events from only one specific calendar?
faq.answer: Use the --calendar flag followed by the calendar name or ID, for example: gws calendar +agenda --calendar 'Work'

faq.question: What timezone does the agenda use?
faq.answer: By default, it uses your Google account's configured timezone. Override with --timezone and an IANA timezone identifier like America/New_York.

faq.question: Can I see past events with this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill only shows upcoming events. The flags (--today, --tomorrow, --week, --days) all look forward from the current time.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The default format is plain text. You can use --format table for a tabular view of events.
